skill.name: iterate-pr
skill.description: Guides an agent through repeated GitHub pull request checks, review feedback, fixes, commits, pushes, and CI monitoring until the PR is ready.
skill.summary: Iterates on a GitHub pull request until CI passes and review feedback is addressed.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown command examples as shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. The command examples are mostly legitimate for a PR automation skill, but the workflow can run GitHub CLI and Git commands, edit files, commit, and push changes. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malware behavior, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External GitHub and Git Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run GitHub CLI and Git commands to inspect PRs, fetch comments, read workflow logs, commit changes, push branches, and watch CI. This is expected for the skill, but it can affect remote pull requests and should require user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit and semantically aligned with PR iteration. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands target GitHub and Git workflows.

medium_findings.title: Automated Repository Modification and Push
medium_findings.description: The workflow tells the agent to make code changes, stage all changes, commit, and push. This can publish unintended local edits if the repository is not clean or if review feedback is interpreted incorrectly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs code modification and push behavior. The risk depends on user approval and repository state, so medium severity is appropriate.

medium_findings.title: Unquoted Branch Name Command Substitution
medium_findings.description: The workflow uses command substitution to pass the current branch into a GitHub CLI command. This is likely intended, but an unusual branch name could split arguments or make the command fail.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell substitution is present in a command example. It is a reliability and argument handling concern, but it does not show malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Scanner Hits
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings appear to be false positives from prose or Markdown examples. No weak cryptographic API, secret handling, host enumeration, or malware-like reconnaissance was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text or normal GitHub workflow instructions. I did not find cryptographic code or system reconnaissance commands in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote PR Inspection Through GitHub CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses GitHub CLI commands to read pull request metadata, checks, reviews, comments, and workflow logs. This is legitimate but depends on authenticated access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and require `gh` authentication. They read repository and CI data but do not show exfiltration outside GitHub.

dangerous_patterns.title: Commit and Push Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to stage all changes, commit, and push. This can change remote repository state and should be used only on the intended branch.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands directly modify repository state. The behavior is expected for the skill and not evidence of malicious intent.

content.user_title: Iterate Pull Requests Until CI Passes
content.value_statement: CI failures and review comments can create a slow fix-check cycle. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through PR inspection, targeted fixes, commits, pushes, and CI monitoring.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the pull request for the current branch with GitHub CLI.
Checks CI status and waits for pending bot checks when needed.
Collects review comments, conversation comments, and bot feedback.
Reads failed GitHub Actions logs before making changes.
Guides targeted fixes, commits, pushes, and repeated CI checks.
limitations: Requires authenticated GitHub CLI access in the repository.
Works only for GitHub pull request workflows.
Cannot resolve unclear review feedback without user input.
Can commit and push changes if the agent is allowed to proceed.

use_cases.title: Fix CI Failures
use_cases.description: Investigate failed checks, read logs, apply targeted fixes, and rerun the PR workflow.

use_cases.title: Address Review Feedback
use_cases.description: Collect human and bot comments, validate each issue, and update the branch.

use_cases.title: Prepare PRs for Merge
use_cases.description: Repeat checks until required CI passes and no actionable feedback remains.

prompt_templates.title: Check My PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of current branch status.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use iterate-pr to inspect the current pull request. Report CI status, review feedback, and the next recommended action before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Failed Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use iterate-pr to read failed CI logs, identify real failures, make minimal fixes, and show me the proposed commit before pushing.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Bot Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use iterate-pr to collect bot comments from Sentry, Codecov, Cursor, Bugbot, and Seer. Validate each comment, then fix only confirmed issues.

prompt_templates.title: Run Full PR Iteration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use iterate-pr to loop through CI failures and review comments until the PR is green. Stop after three repeated failures or any unclear decision.

output_examples.input: Inspect this branch PR and tell me what is blocking merge.
output_examples.output: The PR has two failed checks and one pending bot review. The next step is to read the failed workflow logs before editing.

output_examples.input: Fix the failing tests and continue until CI is green.
output: Read failed workflow logs and found one real test failure.
Applied a targeted fix and prepared a commit summary.
Pushed the branch and restarted CI monitoring.

output_examples.input: Handle all review comments on my PR.
output_examples.output: Three comments were actionable, one was already fixed, and one requires a product decision before code changes.

best_practices: Start from a clean working tree before allowing commits.
Read actual CI logs before changing code.
Ask for approval before pushing changes to shared branches.
anti_patterns: Do not guess failure causes from check names alone.
Do not stage unrelated local changes with the PR fix.
Do not keep retrying the same failure after three attempts.

faq.question: Does this skill require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects authenticated `gh` access in the target repository.

faq.question: Can it work outside GitHub?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uses GitHub pull requests, checks, comments, and Actions logs.

faq.question: Will it automatically push changes?
faq.answer: The workflow includes commit and push steps. Users should require approval before remote changes.

faq.question: How does it handle pending checks?
faq.answer: It waits for important pending checks before processing feedback, especially bot and code analysis checks.

faq.question: Does it trust all review feedback?
faq.answer: No. It tells the agent to validate feedback and skip comments that are not legitimate.

faq.question: When should the agent stop?
faq.answer: It should stop when no PR exists, a rebase is needed, or the same failure persists after three attempts.
